_comment: Table-mode golden for enriching a SCHEMA-ONLY replica of the public bigquery-public-data.ga4_obfuscated_sample_ecommerce dataset (OKF's ga4_merch_store sample). The real export is date-sharded (events_YYYYMMDD); we replicate ONE representative `events` table with the same schema, descriptions stripped, via eval/tools/make_public_replica.py (so enrichment must come from the grounding corpus). Out-of-the-box flow: build the replica, run the agent in table mode against it grounded by eval/corpora/ga4_obfuscated_sample_ecommerce/, then score with `python -m eval --output-dir <out> --golden goldens/table_ga4_obfuscated_sample_ecommerce.json`. Scoring matches each table by NAME. This is OKF's single-denormalized-table sample (contrasts crypto_bitcoin's multi-table FK story): one wide events table with nested/repeated RECORDs. Facts grounded in the GA4 BigQuery Export documentation.
_dataset_doc:
Public SOURCE dataset (date-sharded events_YYYYMMDD). Build a schema-only, description-stripped single-`events` replica in your project with `python eval/tools/make_public_replica.py --source bigquery-public-data.ga4_obfuscated_sample_ecommerce --dataset ga4_obfuscated_sample_ecommerce --tables events_20210131=events --project

`.
grounding: eval/corpora/ga4_obfuscated_sample_ecommerce (2 local markdown docs: dataset overview, events schema/nested records) — pass as --folders to the agent.

run._doc:
Runnable CASE for `python -m eval --run --project

`: build the schema-only replica (one representative events table), then enrich it in table mode grounded by the local corpus. --folders is relative to agents/enrichment.

golden_facts: holds one row per GA4 event from the Google Merchandise Store -- event-based, not session- or pageview-based
event_name identifies the interaction (e.g. page_view, view_item, add_to_cart, begin_checkout, purchase) and event_timestamp is microseconds since epoch
event_params is a repeated key-value record of event-scoped parameters (e.g. page_location, ga_session_id, currency, value) read by UNNEST + filtering on key
user_pseudo_id is the pseudonymous device identifier (the primary key for counting users); user_id is the business user id used to stitch a signed-in user across devices
nested RECORD columns device, geo, and traffic_source capture per-event context (device category/OS, country/city, acquisition source/medium)
ecommerce holds order-level metrics (e.g. purchase_revenue_in_usd, total_item_quantity) and items is a repeated record of the products in the event (item_id, item_name, price, quantity)
a session is reconstructed at query time from user_pseudo_id plus the ga_session_id event parameter -- there is no separate session or order table
